data.content:
The file is generated successfully but never added to the compilation. The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes the `.cs` file but doesn't include it in the `Compile` item group, so Roslyn never sees it.

**Fix:** Add a `<Compile>` item for the generated file inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. By the time `CoreCompile` runs, the compiler doesn't know about the generated file.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, using `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` as an `ItemGroup` inside the target.

Let me verify this by checking the generated file exists:

arguments.new_str:
<!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />"}

data.content: Build succeeds and runs correctly. The problem was that the `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote the `.cs` file to disk but never told MSBuild to compile it. The fix adds an `<ItemGroup><Compile Include="..." /></ItemGroup>` inside the target so the generated file is included in the Roslyn compilation.
